build_notes.scope: Full-size crawler excavators only. Operating weight >= 18 metric tons.
build_notes.exclusions: Mini excavators, midi excavators, wheeled excavators, specialty/demolition/long-reach variants.
build_notes.schema_reference: mtm_mini_ex_registry_v1.json — structure and naming only. No specs, models, or defaults inherited.
field_changes_vs_mini_ex: blade_width_in — REMOVED (not applicable to standard excavators)
build_notes.conservative_policy: Prefer null over wrong. Prefer manual_review over locked. No guessed values marked locked.

lock_pass.locked_by: MTM registry lock pass — Phase 1A complete
lock_pass.note_fix_applied: deere_210_p_tier notes[0] — removed contradictory "introduced 2022"; aligned to 2021 per years_supported and YEAR_NOTE

patch_notes: patch1 (2026-03-24): Added cat_308_cr — Top50 scope exception. 8-tonne midi class, below Phase 1A threshold but included for market coverage. spec_confidence=LOW; all spec fields null pending OEM PDF confirmation.

patch2 (2026-03-25): cat_308_cr OEM spec lock. Source: AEHQ8163-01 (03-2019). Engine corrected C2.8->C3.3B. Locked: hp(70.3 net/74.3 gross), OW(17,960 min), flow(44gpm), pressure(4,134psi), dig_depth(162in std), bucket_BF(13,946lbf), travel(1.9/3.2mph), fuel(39gal). spec_confidence->HIGH.

notes: SCOPE: 320GC is the value-spec variant of the Cat 320 Next Generation platform introduced in 2017. Not to be confused with earlier 320D or 320E generations.
EXCEPTION: horsepower_hp null — CAT OEM publishes gross HP only for this model; net HP not separately published.
SOURCE: C4.4 engine confirmed per CAT OEM spec sheet. Displacement 268 cu in (4.4L). Turbocharged, Tier 4 Final.
NOTE: aux_flow and aux_pressure are package_dependent — standard equipped with boom/stick circuits only; hydraulic thumbs and attachments require optional auxiliary circuit kit.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not consistently published by CAT for this model; set null pending manual verification.
YEAR_NOTE: years_supported.end set to 2022 — Cat 320 Next Generation (GC variant) produced 2017–2022 before transition to Next Gen 2 platform. Verify exact production cutoff per serial range.
SCOPE: 320EL is the extended undercarriage variant of the Cat 320E generation (2013–2017). Distinct from 320GC which belongs to the Next Generation (2017+) platform.
EXCEPTION: horsepower_hp null — CAT OEM publishes gross HP only; net HP figure not separately confirmed.
SOURCE: C6.6 engine, 6-cylinder turbocharged, 403 cu in (6.6L), Tier 4 Interim confirmed per CAT OEM spec sheet for E-series generation.
NOTE: EL (extended length) undercarriage adds stability in deep-dig and slope applications; operating weight reflects EL configuration.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not consistently published by CAT for this generation; set null pending manual verification.

source_refs: John Deere 210G LC OEM spec sheet — deere.com
notes: SCOPE: 210G LC is the G-Series generation of the John Deere 210 platform. Not to be confused with the earlier 210D or later 210P-Tier generations.
SOURCE: John Deere 4045H engine, 4-cylinder, turbocharged, 275 cu in (4.5L), Tier 4 Interim confirmed per JD OEM spec sheet.
NOTE: John Deere publishes rated (net) HP as the primary figure; horsepower_gross_hp not separately published — set null.
NOTE: LC designation = long carriage undercarriage; operating weight reflects LC configuration.
NOTE: aux_flow and aux_pressure package_dependent — supplemental hydraulics for attachment use require optional circuit.

source_refs: John Deere 210 P-Tier OEM spec sheet — deere.com
notes: SCOPE: 210 P-Tier is the current-generation John Deere 210 platform introduced in 2021. Supersedes the 210G LC. Tier 4 Final emissions.
SOURCE: John Deere publishes net HP as primary figure; gross HP not separately published — set null.

NOTE: engine_model MEDIUM confidence — JD P-Tier documentation references 4.5L 4-cylinder engine consistent with 4045H family, but exact sub-variant designation not independently confirmed on P-Tier OEM sheet. Set manual_review.
NOTE: Dimensions highly consistent with 210G LC generation — verified as same platform footprint.
NOTE: aux_flow and aux_pressure package_dependent — supplemental hydraulic circuit not standard.
YEAR_NOTE: years_supported.start corrected to 2021 — JD 210 P-Tier introduced at Conexpo 2021, production units available mid-2021. Earlier entry of 2022 was overly conservative.

source_refs: Komatsu PC210 LC-11 OEM spec sheet — komatsu.com
notes: SCOPE: PC210 LC-11 is the -11 generation of the Komatsu PC210 platform, introduced 2019. Generation suffix -11 is part of the official model designation and must be preserved in model_slug.
SOURCE: SAA4D107E-3 engine confirmed per Komatsu OEM spec sheet. 4-cylinder, turbocharged, 256 cu in (4.2L), Tier 4 Final.
NOTE: Komatsu publishes net (SAE) HP as primary figure; gross HP not separately published in OEM sheet — set null.
NOTE: LC designation = long carriage; operating weight reflects LC undercarriage configuration.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not confirmed in Komatsu OEM documentation at time of build; set null pending manual verification.

WARNING: SANY OEM English documentation is inconsistent in precision and completeness. All spec values sourced from secondary references and should be treated as MEDIUM confidence pending OEM primary confirmation.

NOTE: engine_model null — SANY does not consistently publish specific engine model strings in English-language documentation. Isuzu 4-cylinder turbocharged engine family confirmed at manufacturer level only.
NOTE: emissions_tier null — SANY US market SY215 units have been sold under varying emissions compliance statuses depending on year and market. Verify per serial number before locking.
NOTE: SANY SY215 operating weight confirmed ≥ 18 metric tons (~21.5 MT); qualifies as full-size excavator for registry scope.
NOTE: All MEDIUM-confidence values sourced from RitchieSpecs and IronPlanet listings; require OEM primary source confirmation before behavior can be upgraded to locked.

CAB_NOTE: cab_type set to MEDIUM/manual_review — SANY US market SY-series units have been delivered in both enclosed cab and canopy configurations depending on year and order spec. Verify per individual unit before locking.
WARNING: SANY OEM English documentation is inconsistent. All performance specs sourced from secondary references; require OEM primary confirmation before any field behavior can be upgraded to locked.
NOTE: engine_model null — SANY does not consistently publish specific Isuzu engine sub-variant strings. 6-cylinder turbocharged confirmed at engine family level only.
NOTE: emissions_tier null — US market SANY units vary by year and import batch; verify per serial number.
NOTE: SY265 operating weight ~26 MT; fully within full-size excavator scope. No mini/midi exclusion risk.
NOTE: track_width_in 27.6 in reflects wider undercarriage vs. 20-ton class — consistent with 26T class machines.

CAB_NOTE: cab_type set to MEDIUM/manual_review — SANY US market SY-series units have been delivered in both enclosed cab and canopy configurations depending on year and order spec. Verify per individual unit before locking.
WARNING: SANY OEM English documentation inconsistency applies to this record. All specs sourced from secondary references; require OEM primary confirmation before locking.
NOTE: engine_model null — Cummins engine family confirmed at manufacturer level; specific model designation not independently confirmed from English OEM documentation.
NOTE: SY365 is a 35-ton class machine (~37.6 MT). Well within full-size excavator scope. No exclusion concerns.
NOTE: emissions_tier null — US market SANY vary by year and import batch; verify per serial number.
NOTE: SY365 uses wider 31.5 in steel track and larger fuel capacity consistent with 35-ton class — not to be confused with 20-ton class specs.

CAB_NOTE: cab_type set to MEDIUM/manual_review — SANY US market SY-series units have been delivered in both enclosed cab and canopy configurations depending on year and order spec. Verify per individual unit before locking.

source_refs: Volvo EC350 OEM spec sheet — volvoce.com
notes: SCOPE: EC350 is the standard undercarriage variant. EC350EL is a separate record — do NOT merge. Undercarriage length, operating weight, and ground bearing pressure differ between variants.
SOURCE: Volvo D8J engine confirmed per Volvo CE OEM spec sheet. 6-cylinder turbocharged, 488 cu in (8.0L), Tier 4 Final.
NOTE: Volvo publishes gross HP as primary figure; net HP not separately published — set null.
NOTE: EC350 is a 35-ton class machine (~35.8 MT). Well within full-size excavator scope.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not published in Volvo CE OEM spec sheet for this model; set null pending manual verification.

source_refs: Volvo EC350EL OEM spec sheet — volvoce.com
notes: SCOPE: EC350EL is the extended length undercarriage variant of the EC350. Registered as a separate record from EC350 — operating weight, dig depth, and reach figures differ.
SOURCE: D8J engine, same as EC350 standard — confirmed per Volvo CE OEM spec sheet. Powertrain and hydraulic circuit identical between EC350 and EC350EL.
NOTE: EL undercarriage adds ~2,600 lbs operating weight vs. standard EC350 and extends dig depth and reach.
NOTE: Volvo publishes gross HP as primary figure; net HP not separately published — set null.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not published in Volvo CE OEM spec sheet; set null pending manual verification.

VARIANT_NOTE: overall_width_in, track_width_in, and ground_clearance_in are intentionally identical to standard variant — EL (extended length) adds undercarriage length fore-aft only; machine width and ground clearance are unchanged. Values confirmed defensible per OEM documentation.

source_refs: Volvo EC380 OEM spec sheet — volvoce.com
notes: SCOPE: EC380 is the standard undercarriage variant. EC380EL is a separate record. Do NOT merge — weight and reach figures differ.
SOURCE: Volvo D11 engine confirmed per Volvo CE OEM spec sheet. 6-cylinder turbocharged, 671 cu in (11.0L), Tier 4 Final. Larger displacement than EC350 D8J.
NOTE: Volvo publishes gross HP as primary figure; net HP not separately published — set null.
NOTE: EC380 is a 40-ton class machine (~40 MT). Well within full-size excavator scope.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not published in Volvo CE OEM spec sheet; set null pending manual verification.

source_refs: Volvo EC380EL OEM spec sheet — volvoce.com
notes: SCOPE: EC380EL is the extended length undercarriage variant. Separate record from EC380 — operating weight and reach differ. Do NOT merge.
SOURCE: D11 engine, same as EC380 standard — confirmed per Volvo CE OEM spec sheet. Powertrain identical between EC380 and EC380EL.
NOTE: EL undercarriage adds ~2,200 lbs operating weight vs. standard EC380 and extends dig depth and reach.
NOTE: Volvo publishes gross HP as primary figure; net HP not separately published — set null.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not published in Volvo CE OEM spec sheet; set null pending manual verification.

VARIANT_NOTE: overall_width_in, track_width_in, and ground_clearance_in are intentionally identical to standard variant — EL (extended length) adds undercarriage length fore-aft only; machine width and ground clearance are unchanged. Values confirmed defensible per OEM documentation.

source_refs: Case CX350D OEM spec sheet — casece.com
notes: SCOPE: CX350D is the D-Series generation of the Case CX350 platform. Distinct from the earlier CX350 base model and CX350B/C generations. D-Series introduced Tier 4 Final emissions.
SOURCE: FPT Industrial (CNH Group) engine, 6-cylinder turbocharged, 402 cu in (6.6L), Tier 4 Final confirmed per Case CE OEM spec sheet.

NOTE: engine_model MEDIUM confidence — F4HFE613 is consistent with Case CX350D documentation but exact sub-variant suffix may vary by market region; set manual_review pending primary OEM confirmation.
NOTE: Case publishes gross HP as primary figure; net HP not separately published — set null.
NOTE: counterweight_clearance_in not confirmed in Case CE OEM documentation; set null pending manual verification.

records.classification_note: Falls between mini excavator and full excavator coverage bands. Operating weight ~8 metric tonnes — below Phase 1A scope threshold (18 metric tons). Included as scope exception: Top50 market volume and MTM used-market relevance. Do NOT re-classify to mini_excavator; retain equipment_type=excavator.
source_refs: CAT official spec sheet AEHQ8163-01 (03-2019) — Hawthorne CAT, North America/Europe/ANZP
Engine: Cat C3.3B confirmed. Net HP 70.3 (SAE J1349/ISO 9249). Gross 74.3 (ISO 14396).
OW 17,960 lb = min config (rubber tracks, no counterweight, std stick). Max config 20,678 lb (steel+pads, counterweight, long stick).
Dig depth 162 in = std stick. Long stick: 183 in (15.25 ft). Bucket BF 13,946 lbf. Arm DF 9,509 lbf (std stick).
notes: SCOPE EXCEPTION: Cat 308 is the only sub-18t machine in this registry. Phase 1A scope was >=18 metric tons. Cat 308 operating weight ~8 metric tons (17,640 lb). Added because it is a Top50 slot and has high used-market volume per EDA / MachineryTrader.
CLASSIFICATION: 'CR' suffix = Compact Radius (counterweight stays within machine width on full rotation). NOT zero-tail-swing — do not conflate. tail_swing_type locked to 'compact_radius'.
FAMILY: '308' covers 308E2 CR (2015–2019, C2.8T engine) and 308 CR Next Gen (2020+). Specs differ between generations. Do not auto-fill without confirming generation.
engine_model: Cat C2.8 applies to 308E2 CR and 308 CR generation. MEDIUM confidence — confirm displacement and exact suffix before lock.
emissions_tier: Tier 4 Final for 2015+ production (C2.8T engine). MEDIUM — set manual_review until OEM PDF confirms for exact serial range.
track_type: Rubber tracks standard on 308 CR; steel available as option. MEDIUM — confirm configuration before listing.
PRIORITY: Pull Cat 308E2 CR or 308 CR Next Gen OEM spec sheet (AEHQ-series) to populate operating_weight_lbs, HP, dig depth, reach, and hydraulic specs before first listing.
REGISTRY ORIGIN: Added 2026-03-24 to close Top50 gap. Previously absent from all registries — confirmed missing by direct slug search.
